description.en: If something is wrong with the payment response's data (and there is a recoverable error), this method allows a merchant to request that the user retry the payment. The method takes an object as argument, which is used to signal to the user exactly what is wrong with the payment response so they can try to correct any issues.

description.en: A string giving the name of the recipient, purchaser, or contact person at the payment address.

excerpt.es: A string giving the name of the recipient, purchaser, or contact person at the payment address.
excerpt.en: A string giving the name of the recipient, purchaser, or contact person at the payment address.

description.en: A string containing the top level administrative subdivision of the country, for example a state, province, oblast, or prefecture.

excerpt.en: A string containing the top level administrative subdivision of the country, for example a state, province, oblast, or prefecture.

description.en: Returns the identifier of thePaymentRequestthat produced the current response. This is the same value supplied in thePaymentRequest()constructor bydetails.id.

excerpt.es: Returns the identifier of thePaymentRequestthat produced the current response. This is the same value supplied in thePaymentRequest()constructor bydetails.id.
excerpt.en: Returns the identifier of thePaymentRequestthat produced the current response. This is the same value supplied in thePaymentRequest()constructor bydetails.id.

description.en: A string that specifies the relying party's identifier (for example "login.example.org").

excerpt.es: A string that specifies the relying party's identifier (for example "login.example.org").
excerpt.en: A string that specifies the relying party's identifier (for example "login.example.org").
